summary: Drew DeVault announces sway 0.15-rc1 as the final pre-1.0 release and introduces wlroots, a new low-level library replacing wlc for Wayland compositing. While wlc abstracted away most compositor plumbing, its limitations had become blockers for sway's development. Wlroots gives compositors much more control over low-level components at the cost of additional complexity. Development on wlroots has progressed rapidly thanks to motivated contributors, and integration with sway is now underway. The migration will unlock long-requested features like rotated displays, fractional scaling, and multi-GPU support. DeVault positions wlroots as a shared platform for the broader Wayland ecosystem.
key_points: wlroots replaces wlc as sway's compositor plumbing library, trading ease of use for much greater power and customizability
The migration will enable previously impossible features including rotated displays, fractional scaling, multi-GPU support, and drawing tablet support
wlroots is designed as a shared platform for the Wayland ecosystem, with other compositors like way-cooler already planning to adopt it
Community contributors have been essential to wlroots development, writing and maintaining entire subsystems
Sway 1.0 is targeted for release before the end of 2017
money_quotes: The relationship between wlc and wlroots is similar to the relationship between Pango and Harfbuzz - wlroots is much more powerful, but at the cost of putting a lot more work on the shoulders of sway.
Some of these features are unique to sway even among Wayland and Xorg desktops combined!
wlroots is such a radical change that I can't even begin to imagine all of the features we're going to be able to build.
wlroots is a platform upon which we intend to develop and promote open standards that will unify the extensibility of all Wayland desktops.

All of this work has been possible thanks to a contingent of highly motivated contributors who have done huge amounts of work for wlroots, writing and maintaining entire subsystems far faster than I could have done it alone.
key_insight: By replacing the limited wlc abstraction with the more powerful wlroots library, sway can unlock features impossible under the old architecture while building a shared foundation for the entire Wayland compositor ecosystem.
